sections.heading: Complications
sections.content: Disease-related complications: Uncontrolled pain (management: Palliative radiotherapy (hypofractionated RT: e.g., 8 Gy x 1, 20 Gy x 4-5, or 30 Gy x 10 fractions) and systemic therapy as indicated.24), Impending pathologic fracture (management: Surgery (in patients with good performance status) and/or radiotherapy.24), Impending spinal cord compression (management: Palliative radiotherapy.24), Psychosocial distress (management: Empathetic discussion, support, and counseling by the oncology team and specialized services. Refer to NCCN Guidelines for Distress Management.124), Symptomatic brain metastases (management: Treatment per NCCN Guidelines for Central Nervous System Cancers.10). Treatment-related complications: Chemotherapy: Hematologic toxicities (neutropenia, anemia, thrombocytopenia) common with platinum-based and combination regimens (e.g., carboplatin/paclitaxel, gemcitabine/cisplatin).140,151. Significant grade 3/4 leukopenia (21%), neutropenia (33%), anemia (25%), thrombocytopenia (20%) with irinotecan/carboplatin.174. Overall higher toxicity with addition of etoposide to paclitaxel/carboplatin.141. Immunotherapy: Treatment-related adverse events (any grade) in 64.8% of patients (14.6% grade 3–5) with pembrolizumab.188. Common adverse events: fatigue, anemia, diarrhea, nausea. Immune-mediated adverse events (e.g., pneumonitis, colitis, hepatitis, endocrinopathies) can occur with pembrolizumab and dostarlimab-gxly.188,192,193. Targeted therapy: Serious treatment-related adverse events in 40% of patients with selpercatinib; most common grade 3+: hypertension (22%), increased ALT (16%), increased AST (13%).195. Grade 3–4 adverse events (e.g., febrile neutropenia) can occur with fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan-nxki.201. Surgery: Surgical resection risks are not specified but are inherent to the procedure. Radiation: Chronic radiation-associated dysphagia (7% of patients) in head and neck CUP.235. Radiation-associated toxicities: grade 1 xerostomia, dysphagia, neck stiffness, trismus.236.
